titleStmt.title: National Library of Sweden, A 30
titleStmt.sponsor: Medieval Latin Manuscripts in the National Library: Cataloguing and Digitization
titleStmt.funder: Riksbankens jubileumsfond – The Swedish Foundation for Humanities and Social Sciences

publicationStmt.publisher: manuscripta.se: A Digital Catalogue of Medieval and Early Modern Manuscripts in Sweden

msDesc.head: Book of Hours of undetermined Use

collation.p: Parchment guards visible in all quires.

supportDesc.condition: About a third of the leaf torn.

p.note: Top of page in brown ink by a modern hand: notes about the original wooden covers, replaced in the 19th century. The note also mentions the horn plates that were nailed to the wooden covers and put on the inside of the new covers. Below in black ink: a note describing the new binding made in 1958 by Sven Wiklander. Bottom of page in black ink: a reference to Swarzenski (1936)
In brown ink (rubrics and initials in red), by a medieval hand: fragment of a monastic lectionary.

binding.p: Rebound in 1958 by Sven Wiklander. Brown leather over wooden boards; 2 new clasps.

decoNote.p:
: a badly damaged double framed miniature (now flattened out but formerly wrinkled, probably as a result of the fire in ). The miniature was covered by plexi glass in , but was originally protected by a transparent horn plate fastened by strips of silver or horn, small pieces of which are now attached to the inner face of the box. This practice is mentioned by Swarzenski (1936)
. The miniature shows St. Bartholomew, holding a book in his right hand. Sanctus Bartholomeus inscribed at the top of the frame. Outer fields: at the top the busts of the apostles James and Thomas with name scrolls, at the bottom the busts of Philip the Apostle with a name scroll and unidentified apostle (scroll probably lost); along the sides quatrefoils in a double row of lozenges.

origin.p:
Germany , Bremen , 13th century, first half. The Calendar suggests an origin in Bremen, with mentions of e.g. St Suitbert (8 May), Sts Cantius, Cantianus and Cantianilla (31 May), Margaret the Virgin (13 July), cf. Kroos (1964)
. In the old catalogue, the librarian Oscar Wieselgren has pointed out that the litany of saints mentions e.g. St. Willehad, bishop of Bremen on . This theory is repeated in Gyllene böcker
Engelhart (2004) supports the theory that the volume was produced in North or North west Germany. Swarzenski (1936) suggests a Franconian origin due to the decoration and binding.

provenance.p: The words S. Olaui on , a 15th century addition, suggests a Scandinavian ownership in the late Middle Ages. The current signum, A 30, was first established in Helin’s catalogue from the 1720s (U 91:2:2). The volume is also mentioned in the following catalogue: U 90:4, p. 48, 135. Liber precum in membr. Latine.

acquisition.p: From the Antiquities Archive to the National Library in .

msItem.title: Rhymed hymn
msItem.incipit: O maria piissima stella maris clarissima
msItem.explicit: regnaturus cum domino sine ullo termino amen
msItem.note: This text is most likely a contemporary addition. At the bottom of the page is written in red ink: Deus

note.p: : Sts Cantius, Cantianus and Cantianilla (31 May)

msItem.title: Matins
msItem.incipit: Domine labia mea aperies In honore beatissime marie uirginis iubilemus domino
msItem.explicit: Adiuuabit eam deus uultu suo. Deus in medio eius non commouebitur

note.p:
Antiphon: Benedicta tu in mulieribus, CAO
.

Antiphon: Sicut mirra electa odorem dedisti suauitatis, CAO
.

Antiphon: Ante thorum huius uirginis, CAO
.

First lesson: In omnibus requiem quiesiui, and Responsory: Sancta et immaculata, CAO
.

Second lesson: Et sic in syon, and Responsory: Beata es maria, CAO
.

Third lesson: Quasi cedrus, and Responsory: Felix namque es, CAO
.

Adiuuabit eam deus in uulto suo, CAO
.
Prayer: Deus qui de beate marie uirginis utero
Commemorations of the Cross, St Peter, St Paul, St Nicholas.

msItem.title: Sext
msItem.incipit: consummationis uidi finem latum mandatum tuum nimis.
msItem.explicit: a cunctis hostibus redde securos. Et cetera

note.p: Psalm 118 continued; the word Omnis in the beginning has been omitted by the scribe (Omnis consummationis vidi finem).

msItem.title: Nones
msItem.explicit: filij tui domini dei nostra intercessione saluemur

note.p: Prayer: Deus qui salutis eterne beate marie

msItem.title: Seven Penitential Psalms
msItem.incipit: Domine ne in furore tuo arguas me

msItem.title: Litany
msItem.incipit: Kyrieleison Christeleison Christe audi nos Saluator mundi adiuua nos
msItem.explicit: Domine exaudi orationem meam et clamor meus ad te ueniat

msItem.title: Prayers
msItem.explicit: ut tue consolationis gratiam inuenire ualeamus per dominum
msItem.finalRubric: Anime omnium fidelium defunctorum requiescant in pace amen

msItem.title: Office for the Dead
msItem.incipit: Dilexi quoniam exaudiet dominus uocem orationis mee

note.p: Eighth lesson: Ecce misterium uobis dico
Psalm 50; 64; 62; Canticle of Hezekiah (Ego dixi); Psalm 148; Canticle of Zachary (Benedictus dominus); Psalm 145; prayers.

supportDesc.condition:
: Upper part of the leaves torn, but no text loss.
: Holes resulting in text loss. Parchment torn throughout the manuscript.

p.note: Bottom of page: Royal Library stamp.
Top of page, in black ink by a 13th or first half of 14th century hand: Regine
Top of page, in black ink by a 13th or first half of 14th century hand: et marcellini
Bottom of page, in red ink by a 15th century hand: S olaui
Top of page, in black ink by a 13th or first half of 14th century hand (text lost due to trimming): vii. ma Stephani
Top of page, in black ink by a 13th or first half of 14th century hand: Duorum ewaldorum
Top of page, in black ink by a 13th or first half of 14th century hand: Eustachii
Top of page, in light brown ink, by a 13th or first half of 14th century hand: Salve regina misericordie
Bottom of page, in black ink, by a medieval hand, near contemporary to the main text: integram
Left margin, in black ink, by a medieval hand, near contemporary to the main text: mea
Prayers added by a 14th or 15th century hand, starting: Sancta Maria virgo virginum.

decoDesc.p: The manuscript is decorated throughout. The embellishments around some of the larger initials have been added at a later date. For further description see also Lindqvist Sandgren.

decoNote.p: : Dentelle, knotwork initial D over about 15 lines. Plain initials over 1 line in red and blue. Plain initials over ca 2-7 lines in gold, red, blue and green, several with pen-flourishing and drawings added at a later date, for example on , , .
In circles over about 10 lines: Aquarius , Pisces , Aries , Taurus , Gemini , Cancer , Leo , Virgo , Libra , Scorpio , Sagittarius , Capricorn .

On , miniature Nativity scene in gold, blue, green, red, white and brown. Mary reclining in bed, looking at the newborn Christ-child in the manger, Joseph sitting on the other side, the heads of the ox and the ass behind the manger.
